meta.seed_scenario: One person made a joke at the other’s expense in public, leading to embarrassment and hurt, even after being asked to stop.
meta.selected_scenario: One neighbor makes a light-hearted comment about the other's yard work, but it comes off as condescending, causing irritation.

script.speaker: David
script.text: Hey Huy, I gotta say, your yard is starting to look like a jungle! You might want to do something about those weeds.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: Right, because I’ve been meaning to take advice from the guy who thinks mowing once a month is cutting it.

script.speaker: David
script.text: Oh come on, it was just a joke! You know I didn’t mean anything by it.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: Yeah, but it’s a bit annoying when you keep bringing it up. I get it, I’m not a gardening expert.

script.speaker: David
script.text: I’m just trying to lighten the mood! You know we all have our strengths... and weaknesses.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: Sure, but it feels like you only notice my yard work. What about your fence? It’s about to fall over.

script.speaker: David
script.text: That’s different! I’m working on it. I’ve had some busy weeks.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: So have I. I’m just trying to keep my space nice, you know? I thought neighbors helped each other out.

script.speaker: David
script.text: Alright, I get it. I’ll back off. I just thought it was a little funny, that’s all.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: Well, maybe save the jokes for when we’re not standing in my yard. Just a thought.

script.speaker: David
script.text: Fine, no more jokes about your jungle. Let’s just keep it friendly.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: Yeah, that would be nice.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone seeks a calm environment to recharge, but the other brings in chaos—noise, drama, interruptions—without considering that the other person needs.
meta.selected_scenario: Someone is attempting to meditate in their room, but the other brings in friends for a raucous game night, disrupting the calm.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: Hey, Emma, could you keep it down a bit? I’m trying to meditate.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Meditate? It's game night! You knew we were having friends over.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: Yeah, but I thought it would be quieter. I really need some peace.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I get that, but it’s just one night. Can't you just take a break? You can meditate later.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: It’s not the same. I was really looking forward to this time to recharge. It helps me focus.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Come on, it’s just a few hours. You can’t just hide away all the time.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: I’m not hiding. I just need this. It’s how I deal with everything else going on.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Fine, but I just wish you’d join us sometimes. It feels like you’re always in your room.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: And I wish you’d consider that I need my space now and then. Can you at least keep it down a little?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Okay, okay. I’ll try to keep it down, but I’m not making any promises.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: Thanks. I appreciate it. I’ll join the next game night.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Alright, deal. But you better bring snacks!

meta.seed_scenario: One person initiates physical affection or intimacy, and the other pulls away or makes them feel ashamed for wanting it.
meta.selected_scenario: One person expresses a desire for more intimacy, while the other responds with indifference or a dismissive comment about their needs.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Hey, you wanna watch a movie later? I was thinking we could cuddle up on the couch.

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: Sure, but I thought you were going to finish that project? You’ve been putting it off.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: I can finish it after the movie. Just need a little break. I miss us being close, you know?

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: I get that, but you always want to take breaks. When do you plan to actually work on it?

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: It’s just one night. I thought we could use some time together. It feels like we haven’t done that in ages.

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: Yeah, but it’s not like I can ignore my stuff either. I don’t want to feel guilty for wanting to relax.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: I’m not trying to make you feel guilty! I just don’t want to feel like we’re drifting apart.

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: I don’t think we are. You’re being dramatic.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Dramatic? I just want to show you I care. Is that really so bad?

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: It’s not bad, but it’s also not realistic if you don’t want to deal with your responsibilities.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Fine, I’ll do the project first. But can we at least make time after that? Please?

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: Sure, but don’t expect me to just drop everything for a movie. It doesn’t work like that.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone feels excluded when decisions are made in a shared group without consulting them, despite their ongoing involvement.
meta.selected_scenario: One opponent makes unilateral decisions about a shared project, leaving the other feeling sidelined despite their contributions.

script.speaker: Seung
script.text: So, I just saw your email about the project. You decided we’re going with that supplier without discussing it first?

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: Yeah, I figured it was better to just move forward. We’ve been dragging our feet for weeks.

script.speaker: Seung
script.text: Dragging our feet? I thought we were still weighing options. I was looking into a couple of other suppliers.

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: Right, but we can't wait forever. The deadline is looming, and we need to make a choice.

script.speaker: Seung
script.text: But that’s exactly my point. We should have made that choice together. I’m involved in this too.

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: I get that, but it felt like we were stuck in discussions that weren’t leading anywhere. I thought taking action was better.

script.speaker: Seung
script.text: Taking action without consulting me doesn’t feel like teamwork. It feels like you’re just sidelining my input.

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: I didn’t mean to sideline you, Seung. I just thought we would have more discussions if we had a direction to work with.

script.speaker: Seung
script.text: It’s not just about direction. It’s about respect for everyone's input. I’ve been putting in just as much work.

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: Okay, fair point. But can we focus on the outcome? We need to deliver something solid.

script.speaker: Seung
script.text: Sure, but we can’t just steamroll over each other's contributions. Let’s figure this out together.

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: Alright, let’s sit down and go through the options. Just... next time, you need to speak up sooner.

meta.seed_scenario: One person dismisses or mocks the other's long-term aspirations, suggesting they are unrealistic or unimportant.
meta.selected_scenario: One person expresses excitement about a new opportunity, but the other responds with skepticism, suggesting it might be a waste of time.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Hey Marisol, I just got an email about that startup incubator program I mentioned. They want me to pitch my idea next week!

script.speaker: Marisol
script.text: Oh, that’s... interesting. But are you sure it’s worth your time? I mean, that place gets a ton of applicants.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Yeah, but I think my idea is solid. It’s not just another app, it’s something that could really help small businesses.

script.speaker: Marisol
script.text: I get that, but do you honestly think they'll see that? They probably have way more polished ideas to choose from.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: I know it’s competitive, but I’ve put a lot of work into this. I can’t just back off because it seems tough.

script.speaker: Marisol
script.text: I’m not saying back off, I just don’t want you to get your hopes up. It could be a waste of time if they don’t even give you a chance.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Well, isn’t it better to try and fail than to never try at all? I mean, that’s how you get somewhere, right?

script.speaker: Marisol
script.text: True, but you’ve got to think about what happens if it doesn’t go well. You’ve got deadlines on the current project.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: I can manage both. I just need to put in some extra hours. I’ve done it before.

script.speaker: Marisol
script.text: Okay, but if you're stretching yourself too thin, don’t come to me complaining about it later.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Fair enough. Just support me a little, will you? I really believe in this.

script.speaker: Marisol
script.text: Alright, I’ll back you up, but I still think you’re being a bit too optimistic.

meta.seed_scenario: One person goes through a difficult time but feels the other isn’t showing up—whether emotionally, practically, or with their presence.
meta.selected_scenario: A subordinate seeks guidance during a challenging project but feels their superior is too busy to provide meaningful support.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: David, I really need your input on the marketing strategy for next quarter. The deadline's coming up, and I feel like I'm stuck.

script.speaker: David
script.text: Yeah, I know, Trinh. I've been swamped with the merger stuff. Can you send me a quick summary of where you are?

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: I did send that last week. I thought we could go over it together. I don’t want to just keep guessing.

script.speaker: David
script.text: I get that, but right now, I’m juggling a lot. Can’t you just decide on a direction and I’ll back you up?

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: That’s what I’m trying to avoid! If I make the wrong call and it flops, it’s going to look bad on both of us.

script.speaker: David
script.text: I understand, but I really can’t drop everything right now. I’ll try to look at it tonight, okay?

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Tonight? I can’t wait that long. I need you in the loop, not just as a safety net. It feels like I’m handling this alone.

script.speaker: David
script.text: Look, I’m not trying to leave you hanging. Just... give me a couple of hours. I’ll make it a priority.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: A couple of hours would help, but it’s frustrating. We’re a team, and I need you to be more present.

script.speaker: David
script.text: I’ll do better, I promise. Just bear with me a little longer. I’ll focus on this tonight.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Fine. I’ll check in later, but please try to make it a real conversation, not just a quick fix.

script.speaker: David
script.text: I will. Just... hang tight for now.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone refuses to take basic health precautions, causing the other to feel unsafe.
meta.selected_scenario: An elder advises a child to be cautious about a new hobby, but the child dismisses the advice as overprotective.

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: I just got the skateboard! It's so cool. Did you see me do that trick earlier?

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Yeah, I saw, but you need to wear a helmet, Olivia. You could really hurt yourself.

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: I’m fine! I don’t need a helmet for a little ride around the block.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: It’s not just a little ride. You’re doing tricks. And you know how kids can be reckless.

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: Reckless? I’m just having fun. You sound like my dad when he gets all worked up.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: I’m just trying to look out for you. I’ve seen kids fall and really get hurt. It’s not always fun and games.

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: But you’re acting like I’m going to break my arm just by skating. You didn’t wear a helmet when you were my age, right?

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: I didn’t, but that doesn’t mean it was smart. Times are different now, and you need to be careful.

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: I’ll be careful! I promise. It’s just a helmet, but it feels like you don’t trust me.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: It’s not about trust. It’s about being safe. You can still have fun, but you have to be smart about it.

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: Fine, I guess I’ll wear it, but I still think it’s a bit much.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: It’s not too much if it keeps you safe. Just remember that.

meta.seed_scenario: One person continues a behavior that affects the other negatively, even after being asked to change.
meta.selected_scenario: One partner tries to communicate their need for space, but the other insists on being together, disregarding their feelings.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: Hey, can we take a break from watching movies together? I really just want some time to myself tonight.

script.speaker: Mayra
script.text: What? But we always do movie night on Fridays. It’s our thing.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: I know, but I’ve had a long week and I just need to recharge. I promise it’s not because I don’t want to be with you.

script.speaker: Mayra
script.text: It feels like you’re always needing space now. I’ve been looking forward to this all week.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: I get that, but sometimes I just need to be alone to unwind. It doesn't mean I don’t care about us.

script.speaker: Mayra
script.text: But it’s frustrating when I try to plan something special and you just shut it down. I don’t want to keep asking.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: I’m not trying to push you away. I’m just... overwhelmed. Can’t we have a night where we’re not glued to the screen?

script.speaker: Mayra
script.text: I just wish you’d talk to me about this before the plans are already made. It feels like you’re taking away our time.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: I’m sorry. I didn’t mean to ruin anything. Can we maybe compromise? Like, I’ll take some time alone, and then we can do something together after?

script.speaker: Mayra
script.text: I guess that could work. Just... let me know next time before it gets to this point. I like spending time with you.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: I’ll try to do better. Thanks for understanding, Mayra.

script.speaker: Mayra
script.text: Alright. Just don’t make a habit of it, okay?

meta.seed_scenario: One person disrupts the other’s sleep with noise, lights, or inconsiderate timing, even after being asked not to.
meta.selected_scenario: One person makes plans that disrupt the other's schedule, despite knowing they prefer a quieter evening.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Hey, I thought we agreed on a quiet night in? Why are you blasting music?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I just wanted to get in the mood for our movie marathon. It’s not that loud.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Not that loud? I can hear it from the kitchen! I was trying to take a nap before we started.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: You always fall asleep! And besides, I thought you liked this soundtrack. It’s kind of fun.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Fun is great, but I also like to have some energy when we watch movies, not just crash on the couch.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Well, I figured a little energy wouldn’t hurt. It’s not like I’m throwing a party here.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: It just feels a bit inconsiderate, you know? I mean, I asked for a calm night.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I get it, but you could just… I don’t know, join in instead of sleeping all the time.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: I like being with you, but I still need some downtime. Can we meet in the middle?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Alright, I’ll turn it down a bit. But you owe me some movie time after your nap!

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Deal. Just, next time, give me a heads up, okay?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Okay, okay. I promise!

meta.seed_scenario: Someone shares something they found uplifting, and the other responds with cynicism or mockery, dulling the moment.
meta.selected_scenario: Someone opens up about a recent struggle, and the other deflects by making a joke, shifting the focus away from the serious conversation.

script.speaker: Hiro
script.text: So, I was at that meditation retreat last weekend, and it really hit me how much I’ve been overthinking everything. Like, I feel this weight just lifting, you know?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: A meditation retreat? Were there people in robes? I can just picture it now, everyone sitting cross-legged like they're in some Zen cartoon.

script.speaker: Hiro
script.text: Come on, it’s not like that. It was actually really calming. I had this moment where I just... let everything go.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Letting go sounds great, but did you try not thinking for once? Or was it just all incense and chanting?

script.speaker: Hiro
script.text: It’s not a joke, Emma. I’m serious. I’ve been stressed about work, and for the first time in... forever, I felt okay.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Okay, but what’s that even mean? Isn’t it just a temporary break? You’ll be back to stressing over deadlines in no time.

script.speaker: Hiro
script.text: Maybe, but I need that break to reset. I thought sharing it might inspire you too, but...

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Inspire me? By sitting in silence? Please, I can’t see how that solves anything.

script.speaker: Hiro
script.text: It’s more about the mindset. I thought you’d get it. You’re always talking about wanting to improve how you handle things.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Yeah, but I actually want to do something about it, not just sit around pretending it works.

script.speaker: Hiro
script.text: I’m not pretending! It does help. I guess I just expected you to be more supportive.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Supportive? I just don’t want you to think sitting quietly is a miracle cure. That’s all.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone tries to offer 'helpful' advice by steering the other person toward a path they didn’t choose, assuming they know what’s best.
meta.selected_scenario: One sibling expresses excitement about a personal project, but the other dismisses it as a waste of time, insisting they should pursue something more conventional.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: So, I just got an idea for this art project. I want to create a mural on that old wall in the park. It could be, like, a community thing.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: A mural? Seriously? You think people are going to care about that?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Why wouldn’t they? It's about bringing everyone together. Plus, I love painting. It’s not just some hobby.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: But you could be working on your portfolio or something that actually gets you a job. I mean, you need to think practical.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Practical? Jack, my whole life’s not just about getting a job. I want to make something that matters to me.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Yeah, but that wall's just going to end up covered in graffiti, and you’ll waste all that time. Why not enter that graphic design contest instead?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Because it’s not about winning a contest for me. It’s about expressing something real. You never get that.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: I get it, but can’t you just, I don’t know, do both? Focus on stuff that’ll actually pay off.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Why can’t you just support me? I don’t want to spend my life chasing after some boring corporate gig.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: I’m just trying to help. You always talk about struggling financially, and then you want to take on a huge project? It doesn’t add up.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: What doesn’t add up is you not trusting me to figure this out. I know what I want, Jack.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Fine, but don’t come crying to me when it all falls apart.

meta.seed_scenario: During arguments, one person raises their voice or uses sarcasm, making the other feel unsafe or emotionally shut down.
meta.selected_scenario: During a discussion about responsibilities, one person raises their voice, causing the other to withdraw and feel unheard.

script.speaker: David
script.text: Emma, I asked you to take out the trash before dinner. It’s been sitting there all day.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I know, Dad, but I was busy with my homework. It's not a big deal.

script.speaker: David
script.text: Not a big deal? It's part of your responsibilities. You need to start taking this seriously.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I said I’d do it! I just got caught up, okay? You don’t have to yell.

script.speaker: David
script.text: I’m not yelling! I’m just trying to get you to understand that we all have to chip in around here.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Well, when you raise your voice like that, it makes me want to shut down. I can’t think.

script.speaker: David
script.text: I didn’t mean to make you feel that way, but this isn’t the first time. I just don’t get why it’s so hard to follow through.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Maybe because you make it sound like I’m not doing anything at all? It just feels like you don’t trust me.

script.speaker: David
script.text: It’s not about trust; it’s about responsibility. I just want to know you’re taking this seriously.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I am! But if you keep coming at me like this, I’m just going to stop talking about it.

script.speaker: David
script.text: Then let’s find a way to talk without me raising my voice. Can we agree on that?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Yeah, I guess. Just... try to remember I’m still figuring all this out.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone seeks a calm environment to recharge, but the other brings in chaos—noise, drama, interruptions—without considering that the other person needs.
meta.selected_scenario: Someone is attempting to have a serious conversation about project timelines, but the other diverts the topic to personal anecdotes, ignoring the urgency.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Trinh, I wanted to go over the project timelines today. We really need to nail down those deadlines before the meeting next week.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Sure, but did I tell you about the new café that just opened? They have this amazing matcha latte.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: I appreciate that, but it’s kind of urgent. We have to finalize the tasks for the team, and we’re running out of time.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: I know, I know, but seriously, you have to try this place. I went there after work yesterday and ran into Mark. He was telling me all about his trip to Japan.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: That’s great for Mark, but we’re not on a trip here. I need your input on the budget allocations for each phase.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Right, but if we don’t take a break once in a while, we’ll burn out. I thought a little distraction would be good for us.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: I get that, but can we just focus for a bit? We can't afford to fall behind because we’re sidetracked.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Okay, okay. I’ll get back on track. Just thought a light moment would help ease the stress.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: I understand, but we really need to prioritize this right now. Can we set a timer for 15 minutes to go over the timelines?

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Fine, 15 minutes. But you owe me a latte at that new café after this.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Deal. Let’s get this sorted.

meta.seed_scenario: One person made a joke at the other’s expense in public, leading to embarrassment and hurt, even after being asked to stop.
meta.selected_scenario: One neighbor offers unsolicited advice on home improvements, but the other feels their choices are being criticized rather than supported.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Hey Yesenia, I saw you putting up those new shutters. Are they really the color you want? I mean, they seem a bit... bright.

script.speaker: Yesenia
script.text: What’s wrong with bright? I like them, and they match my door.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: I just think it’s a bit much. You know, subtle can be nice too. Not everyone goes for neon.

script.speaker: Yesenia
script.text: Neon? Seriously? It’s not neon, Jack, it’s just a cheerful blue. Maybe you should stick to your own house.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: I’m just trying to help, okay? It’s not about your taste, it’s about resale value. This place is a neighborhood, not a carnival.

script.speaker: Yesenia
script.text: Right, because you’re the expert on resale value now? Last I checked, this isn’t a competition about whose house looks better.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: I’m not saying that! But you’ve got to think about what buyers want. You can’t just pick whatever you feel like.

script.speaker: Yesenia
script.text: Maybe I’ll just keep doing what I like and stop worrying about what you think. My home, my choices.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Fine, but don’t say I didn’t warn you when you’re trying to sell it.

script.speaker: Yesenia
script.text: You know what? I’m not trying to impress anyone here. Just living my life.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Guess we’ll see how that goes then.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone seeks a calm environment to recharge, but the other brings in chaos—noise, drama, interruptions—without considering that the other person needs.
meta.selected_scenario: One person tries to unwind after a long day, but the other insists on hosting loud gatherings, disregarding the need for peace.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Can you keep it down a bit? I’m trying to relax after a long day.

script.speaker: Sofia
script.text: But I invited some friends over. We’re just having some fun!

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: I get that, but it’s really loud. I just need a quiet space to unwind.

script.speaker: Sofia
script.text: I thought you’d be okay with it since you knew I had plans. It’s just one night.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Yeah, but it feels like every other night lately. I can’t catch a break.

script.speaker: Sofia
script.text: I promise it won’t be like this every time. Can’t you just chill for a bit?

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Chill? With the music blasting and people shouting? That’s not really how I recharge.

script.speaker: Sofia
script.text: Okay, but don’t act like I’m throwing a rager or something. We’re just playing some games.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: I know, but I’ve got work tomorrow. I need to be awake and focused.

script.speaker: Sofia
script.text: Fine, I can tell them to keep it down. Just... don’t be a buzzkill every time I want to have people over.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: I’m not trying to ruin your fun, just asking for a little consideration.

script.speaker: Sofia
script.text: Alright, I’ll talk to them. But you owe me a night of fun once things calm down.

meta.seed_scenario: One person initiates physical affection or intimacy, and the other pulls away or makes them feel ashamed for wanting it.
meta.selected_scenario: One person attempts to initiate a romantic gesture, but the other reacts with annoyance, suggesting they should focus on more practical matters.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: Hey, I was thinking... it’s been a while since we had a quiet evening together. How about we light some candles and watch that movie we love?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Really? Now? We have so much laundry to fold, and the kitchen’s a disaster.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: I know, but we could just take a little break. We need to unwind sometimes.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Unwind? We never seem to get anything done. You know that pile in the living room isn’t going to fold itself.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: I get that, but I thought we could use some time for us. It feels like we haven’t connected lately.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I’m not in the mood for that right now. It just feels... impractical? Can’t we focus on what needs to be done?

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: It’s not just about the chores, Emma. I miss being close to you. Can’t we just take an hour?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: You say that, but every time we try to relax, we end up feeling guilty about everything else. It’s exhausting.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: I’m not trying to make you feel guilty! I just thought... I don’t know, maybe it could help us recharge?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I appreciate that, but right now, it feels like we have too much on our plates. I can’t switch gears like that.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: Okay, I hear you. But can we at least agree to take a short break, maybe after the laundry? Just fifteen minutes?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Fine, just fifteen minutes. But after that, we really need to tackle this stuff.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone feels excluded when decisions are made in a shared group without consulting them, despite their ongoing involvement.
meta.selected_scenario: In a meeting, one party presents plans that exclude the other, creating tension over the lack of consultation in the decision-making process.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: So, the plan is set to go live next week without any input from me? I didn’t even get a heads-up.

script.speaker: Maribel
script.text: We discussed this in the last meeting, Daniel. You were there. I thought you were on board.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Yeah, I was there, but that doesn't mean I agreed with everything. We need to talk it through, not just decide things in a vacuum.

script.speaker: Maribel
script.text: I get it, but we can't keep delaying just to revisit every single point. We need to move forward.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Forward? By excluding key voices like mine? That doesn’t feel like progress to me.

script.speaker: Maribel
script.text: I didn't exclude you. You were just silent about your concerns then. It's not like I ignored you on purpose.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Silent? I was processing what was being thrown around. I thought we were a team; you should have checked in with me.

script.speaker: Maribel
script.text: This is why we keep missing deadlines. We can’t afford to slow down because someone didn’t share their thoughts.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: And this is why I’m frustrated! It feels like you’re just pushing your agenda without considering what I bring to the table.

script.speaker: Maribel
script.text: It’s not personal, Daniel. I thought you were okay with it, and now we’re stuck in this back-and-forth.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Well, maybe if we actually communicated better, we wouldn’t be stuck.

script.speaker: Maribel
script.text: Fine, then let's figure out how to do that instead of just arguing.

meta.seed_scenario: One person dismisses or mocks the other's long-term aspirations, suggesting they are unrealistic or unimportant.
meta.selected_scenario: One person questions the other's commitment to their career goals, implying they are not serious enough about their future.

script.speaker: James
script.text: So, I saw that you’re still working on that grant proposal for the art project. How serious are you about this?

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: What do you mean? It’s a great opportunity to get some funding and recognition. I thought you’d support it.

script.speaker: James
script.text: I do, I just... I don’t know. It feels a bit out there, doesn’t it? Like, are you really going to make a living off of this?

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Why does it have to be all about making a living? I want to create something meaningful. Isn’t that worth pursuing?

script.speaker: James
script.text: Sure, but you’ve talked about wanting to move up in the company. This seems... like a distraction.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Distraction? It’s part of who I am! I can’t just push my passions aside for a promotion.

script.speaker: James
script.text: I get that, but you’ve been putting in a lot of hours here. Are you really managing your time well with all this?

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: I manage just fine, thank you very much. I can balance my work and my dreams. I just wish you’d see that.

script.speaker: James
script.text: I just want you to be realistic about it. It’s great to have dreams, but don’t let them cloud your judgment.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: And I want you to trust that I know what I’m doing. I mean, we all have our paths, right?

script.speaker: James
script.text: Yeah, I guess. I just worry that you’ll regret not being more focused down the line.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: I appreciate your concern, but I have a vision for my future. I just need you to believe in it too.

meta.seed_scenario: One person goes through a difficult time but feels the other isn’t showing up—whether emotionally, practically, or with their presence.
meta.selected_scenario: One person shares their struggles with workload, but the other brushes it off, suggesting they should just manage better.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Lucia, I noticed you’ve been missing deadlines lately. Is everything okay?

script.speaker: Lucia
script.text: Honestly, it’s just been a lot. The project with marketing is eating up all my time, and then there’s this report due next week.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: I get that, but everyone’s busy. You just need to manage your time better. We’ve all got our plates full.

script.speaker: Lucia
script.text: I’m trying, but it feels like I’m the only one juggling all this. I can't even take a lunch break.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: But if you plan it out, you can find the time. I mean, I’m managing my tasks.

script.speaker: Lucia
script.text: Right, but do you know how many late nights I’ve put in just to keep up? It’s exhausting.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: I understand, but you can’t just keep saying it’s too much. You have to push through.

script.speaker: Lucia
script.text: I’m not asking for an easy way out, just some acknowledgment. It feels like you don’t see how hard I’m working.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: I see that, but at the end of the day, it’s about results. You need to deliver.

script.speaker: Lucia
script.text: And I will, but I just need a little support here. It would help if you were a bit more understanding.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: I’ll try, but just know that I need to see progress, too.

script.speaker: Lucia
script.text: Fair enough, but I could use some actual help instead of just pressure.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone refuses to take basic health precautions, causing the other to feel unsafe.
meta.selected_scenario: A child wants to try a new sport without protective gear, while the elder insists on safety measures, leading to a disagreement.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: Come on, Aunt Patricia, it’s just a little skateboarding. I don’t need a helmet.

script.speaker: Patricia
script.text: Huy, you know how many kids fall and get hurt. I’m not okay with you just going out there without any protection.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: But all my friends don’t wear helmets! They say it’s cooler that way.

script.speaker: Patricia
script.text: It's not about cool, it’s about being safe. What if you fall and hit your head? You could really get hurt.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: I’m not going to fall! I’ve been practicing, and I can do tricks now. I promise I’ll be careful.

script.speaker: Patricia
script.text: I get that, but being careful isn’t enough. Remember last summer? You scraped your knee just going down the driveway.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: That was different! I wasn't even going fast.

script.speaker: Patricia
script.text: Exactly! And if you weren't wearing a helmet, it could have been worse. Please just wear it this one time.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: Fine, but I’m only wearing it because you won’t drop it. I still think it’s dumb.

script.speaker: Patricia
script.text: I know, I know, but it’s just for your safety. You can still have fun, just... with a little more protection.

script.speaker: Huy
script.text: Whatever. I’ll wear it, but I’m taking it off the second I get back.

script.speaker: Patricia
script.text: Okay, deal. Just promise me you’ll be careful, alright?

meta.seed_scenario: One person continues a behavior that affects the other negatively, even after being asked to change.
meta.selected_scenario: After one person expresses discomfort with a recurring behavior, the other brushes it off, insisting it's not a big deal.

script.speaker: Huong
script.text: Hey, can we talk about the dishes? I noticed they've been piling up again.

script.speaker: Michael
script.text: It’s just a few plates, Huong. I’ll get to them later.

script.speaker: Huong
script.text: But you said that last week, and it just keeps happening. I thought we agreed to keep up with them together.

script.speaker: Michael
script.text: I know, but I’ve been really busy with work. It's not the end of the world.

script.speaker: Huong
script.text: It feels like it is for me, though. I come home to a messy kitchen every day, and it just adds to my stress.

script.speaker: Michael
script.text: I get it, but I promise I’ll handle it. I just... didn’t think it was that big of a deal.

script.speaker: Huong
script.text: It’s a big deal to me. It shows that we’re not on the same page about sharing responsibilities.

script.speaker: Michael
script.text: Okay, but I’m juggling a lot right now. Can’t you cut me some slack?

script.speaker: Huong
script.text: I am trying to, but it feels like I’m the only one who cares about this stuff. I don’t want to feel like I’m nagging.

script.speaker: Michael
script.text: I didn’t mean to make you feel that way. I’ll get to the dishes tonight, I promise.

script.speaker: Huong
script.text: I just want us to work as a team. It’s not that hard, right?

script.speaker: Michael
script.text: Yeah, you’re right. I’ll do better.

meta.seed_scenario: One person disrupts the other’s sleep with noise, lights, or inconsiderate timing, even after being asked not to.
meta.selected_scenario: After one partner expresses a need for rest, the other continues to play loud music, claiming it's just for fun.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Can you turn that down a bit? I really need some sleep.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: But I’m just having fun! It's a party playlist. It’s not that loud.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: It feels loud to me. I’ve told you a couple of times already. I can’t think straight.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: I mean, come on! It’s the weekend. You said you’d take a nap earlier, right? Just enjoy it.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I did say that, but now I can't even relax. I was looking forward to sleeping.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Okay, but I just wanted to lighten the mood a bit. Can’t we compromise? Just a little longer?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Compromise? You mean crank it up until I’m fully awake? That’s not really fair.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: I didn’t mean to ignore you. It’s just... I thought you’d be okay with it.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Well, I’m not! I feel like I’m always the one adjusting. Can we just keep the volume down?

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Fine, I’ll turn it down. But can we please do something fun later? I don’t want to feel like a buzzkill.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: We can, but it’s also okay to not be loud all the time. Just think about it, alright?

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Yeah, I hear you. I'll try to keep it down. I just want us to have a good time.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone shares something they found uplifting, and the other responds with cynicism or mockery, dulling the moment.
meta.selected_scenario: After a friend expresses excitement about a new hobby, the other quickly compares it to their own past failures, undermining the enthusiasm.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I just started this pottery class, and it's amazing! I feel so inspired when I’m working with the clay, you know?

script.speaker: Malik
script.text: Pottery? Didn’t you say you tried that once before and it was a disaster?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Yeah, but this time it feels different. The instructor is really encouraging, and I actually made a bowl that didn’t collapse!

script.speaker: Malik
script.text: That’s cool, but remember how you got all frustrated last time? Like, it’s just a hobby, right?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Of course it’s just a hobby! But it’s supposed to be fun. I want to enjoy it, not think about past failures.

script.speaker: Malik
script.text: I get that, but maybe you’re just setting yourself up for another letdown. It’s better to be realistic.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Realistic? I’m just trying to enjoy something new! Why do you always have to bring up the past?

script.speaker: Malik
script.text: I’m not trying to ruin it for you! I just don’t want to see you get hurt again. You know how you get.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Well, I appreciate the concern, but I promise I’m going to keep it light this time. Can’t we just celebrate the fact that I made something?

script.speaker: Malik
script.text: Alright, fine. I guess I can try to be supportive. Just... don’t push too hard, okay?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Deal! I’m really excited about it, Malik, I hope you can be too.

script.speaker: Malik
script.text: I’ll work on it. Just don’t expect me to sign up for a class anytime soon.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone tries to offer 'helpful' advice by steering the other person toward a path they didn’t choose, assuming they know what’s best.
meta.selected_scenario: One sibling offers unsolicited advice about a career choice, assuming they know better, while the other feels their autonomy is being undermined.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: I just don’t get why you’re sticking with that photography thing. You’re so good at graphic design—why not just go for that?

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Because I like photography, Wei. It’s what I want to do. Can’t you just support that?

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: Support it? It’s a hobby, Trinh! You need a real career. Design pays better and you have the talent for it.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: It’s not just about the money! I want to capture moments, not just make logos for some company.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: But how will you make a living? You know it’s not like you can just take pictures and expect to pay bills.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: I can figure that out. I have a plan, but you keep acting like you’re the one who knows what’s best for me.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: I’m just trying to help! You’ve been struggling for a while now, and it worries me.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: I appreciate it, but maybe let me decide what I want to do? I’m not a child anymore.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: I get that, but I can’t help but feel like you’re wasting your potential.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: And I can’t help feeling like you’re not trusting me to make my own choices. It’s my life, Wei.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: Fine, but when things get tough, don’t come crying to me.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: I won’t, because I’ll be too busy proving you wrong.

meta.seed_scenario: During arguments, one person raises their voice or uses sarcasm, making the other feel unsafe or emotionally shut down.
meta.selected_scenario: In a moment of frustration, one person resorts to shouting, making the other feel cornered and reluctant to share their feelings.

script.speaker: Tuan
script.text: Olivia, I told you to clean up your room before going out. This isn't a hotel.

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: I know, but I just needed a minute! I can't just drop everything and—

script.speaker: Tuan
script.text: A minute? It’s been three hours! This isn't about you needing a break.

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: Can you just stop yelling for a second? I don't feel safe talking to you when you do that.

script.speaker: Tuan
script.text: I’m not yelling! I’m just trying to get through to you. Why do you always shut down?

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: Because it feels like you don’t care what I think! You just want things done your way.

script.speaker: Tuan
script.text: It's not about my way, it's about taking responsibility! You can’t expect everything to be easy.

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: It’s not easy when you’re shouting. It makes me not want to share anything.

script.speaker: Tuan
script.text: I’m trying to teach you! But if you keep shutting me out, how will you learn?

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: Maybe try talking instead of shouting! It might actually help.

script.speaker: Tuan
script.text: Fine. I’ll try. But you need to understand, this is important.

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: I get that, but we need to figure this out without yelling. I'm willing to try too.

meta.seed_scenario: One person made a joke at the other’s expense in public, leading to embarrassment and hurt, even after being asked to stop.
meta.selected_scenario: One neighbor offers unsolicited advice on home improvements, but the other feels their choices are being criticized rather than supported.

script.speaker: Dat
script.text: Hey Quoc, I was thinking of repainting my fence. What do you think of that color I have in mind?

script.speaker: Quoc
script.text: That color? I mean, it's a bit... bright, don't you think? Maybe something more muted would work better.

script.speaker: Dat
script.text: Bright? I wanted it to stand out a bit. It's just a fence, after all.

script.speaker: Quoc
script.text: Yeah, but it’s right next to my yard. I don’t want it to clash or anything.

script.speaker: Dat
script.text: Clash? It’s just a fence color. I thought we were past caring about that stuff.

script.speaker: Quoc
script.text: I’m just saying, if you’re gonna do it, maybe get a second opinion? Like, from someone who’s actually into design.

script.speaker: Dat
script.text: So you think I can’t decide on my own, huh? I mean, it’s my yard.

script.speaker: Quoc
script.text: No, that’s not what I meant. Just... It’s a big change. You know, I just want you to be sure.

script.speaker: Dat
script.text: I appreciate your concern, but it feels like you’re just criticizing me. I’ve lived here long enough to know what I like.

script.speaker: Quoc
script.text: Alright, fine. Do what you want. Just don’t come crying to me if it ends up looking horrible.

script.speaker: Dat
script.text: Wow, thanks for the support, Quoc. I'll keep that in mind.

script.speaker: Quoc
script.text: I’m just trying to help, Dat.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone shares something they found uplifting, and the other responds with cynicism or mockery, dulling the moment.
meta.selected_scenario: After a friend expresses excitement about a new hobby, the other quickly compares it to their own past failures, undermining the enthusiasm.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: So, I started that pottery class I mentioned! It's actually way more fun than I thought it would be.

script.speaker: Demetrius
script.text: That's cool, man. Pottery? Didn’t you, like, try to make a bowl in high school and it came out all lopsided?

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Yeah, but that was ages ago. I mean, I've learned a thing or two since then. This is different. The instructor is really great.

script.speaker: Demetrius
script.text: Right, but you were super frustrated back then. Don't you think it might just turn out like last time?

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Ugh, I hope not! I’m trying to keep an open mind here. It’s a new experience, you know?

script.speaker: Demetrius
script.text: I get it, I just… I don’t want you to get your hopes up. Remember how bummed you were when it didn’t work out?

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Yeah, but I also learned from that. I mean, isn’t that what hobbies are for? To explore? I’m actually excited!

script.speaker: Demetrius
script.text: I just don’t want to see you disappointed again, man. Pottery's tricky.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Thanks for the concern, but can you just be happy for me? I mean, I need some support here, not a reminder of my past fails.

script.speaker: Demetrius
script.text: Okay, okay, I’ll try. I just worry, you know?

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: I appreciate it. Just try to keep the past out of it, alright? This is a fresh start.

script.speaker: Demetrius
script.text: Fair enough. Let’s see how it goes, then.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone tries to offer 'helpful' advice by steering the other person toward a path they didn’t choose, assuming they know what’s best.
meta.selected_scenario: One sibling offers unsolicited advice about a career choice, assuming they know better, while the other feels their autonomy is being undermined.

script.speaker: Latoya
script.text: So, I was thinking, have you really considered applying for that graphic design internship? I mean, it sounds risky, right?

script.speaker: Ngoc
script.text: What are you talking about? It's exactly what I want to do. I've been planning this for months.

script.speaker: Latoya
script.text: Yeah, but you know how competitive it is. Maybe something more stable would be better. Like, I don't know, teaching or something?

script.speaker: Ngoc
script.text: Teaching? Seriously? That's your idea of a backup plan? I don’t want to be stuck in a classroom just because it’s ‘safe’.

script.speaker: Latoya
script.text: I just think you should be practical, Ngoc. You know how Mom worries about us. What if it doesn’t work out?

script.speaker: Ngoc
script.text: You always do this! You act like I can’t make my own decisions. I’m not asking you to choose for me.

script.speaker: Latoya
script.text: I’m not trying to choose for you! I just... I’d hate to see you fail, that's all.

script.speaker: Ngoc
script.text: It’s not failing if I’m trying something I love! You’re the one who’s scared of taking risks.

script.speaker: Latoya
script.text: And you’re being reckless. There’s a difference!

script.speaker: Ngoc
script.text: Reckless? I call it ambitious. Maybe you should try it sometime instead of just playing it safe.

script.speaker: Latoya
script.text: Ambitious doesn’t pay the bills, Ngoc. You need to think about the long game here.

script.speaker: Ngoc
script.text: Maybe I’m tired of thinking about the long game. I want to enjoy my life now!

meta.seed_scenario: One person goes through a difficult time but feels the other isn’t showing up—whether emotionally, practically, or with their presence.
meta.selected_scenario: A subordinate seeks guidance during a challenging project but feels their superior is too busy to provide meaningful support.

script.speaker: Xuan
script.text: Kimberly, I wanted to talk about the Thompson project. I’m really hitting a wall with the data analysis, and I could use some guidance.

script.speaker: Kimberly
script.text: I understand, Xuan, but I’m swamped right now. I have back-to-back meetings all afternoon.

script.speaker: Xuan
script.text: I get that. It’s just—my deadline is coming up, and I don’t want to fall short. I thought maybe we could just go over my approach quickly?

script.speaker: Kimberly
script.text: Can’t you just tweak what you have? You know the basics. I trust you to figure it out.

script.speaker: Xuan
script.text: I appreciate that, but I feel like I’m missing something crucial. I’ve gone over it a few times, and I’m still stuck.

script.speaker: Kimberly
script.text: Look, I believe in you, but I can't drop everything. You’ve handled tough situations before, right?

script.speaker: Xuan
script.text: Yes, but this one feels different. I don’t want to waste time guessing when I could just clarify a few points.

script.speaker: Kimberly
script.text: I understand. Just… try to manage your time better. Maybe take a break and come back with fresh eyes?

script.speaker: Xuan
script.text: I’ll try, but I really think I need your input to move forward. I don’t want to disappoint you.

script.speaker: Kimberly
script.text: You won't disappoint me, just keep pushing. I’ll check in later if I get a break.

script.speaker: Xuan
script.text: Okay, I’ll do my best. Thanks, I guess.

script.speaker: Kimberly
script.text: Alright, good luck.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone seeks a calm environment to recharge, but the other brings in chaos—noise, drama, interruptions—without considering that the other person needs.
meta.selected_scenario: Someone is attempting to have a serious conversation about project timelines, but the other diverts the topic to personal anecdotes, ignoring the urgency.

script.speaker: Mariela
script.text: Wei, I really need to go over the project timeline before our meeting tomorrow. We might be behind schedule on the deliverables.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: Oh, have you heard about that new café that opened up? I went there last weekend, and they have the best pastries!

script.speaker: Mariela
script.text: That’s nice, but I really need to focus on the timelines right now. If we don’t address this, it could affect the entire project.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: I get that, but I mean, everyone needs a break, right? That café is a great place to recharge.

script.speaker: Mariela
script.text: I understand, but we can’t afford distractions right now. I just need a few minutes to review the numbers.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: Okay, but remember last time we stressed too much? We missed some key points in the report.

script.speaker: Mariela
script.text: I know that, but this isn't just about stress. It's about making sure we meet our deadlines.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: Alright, let’s just take a moment. Once we sort this out, we can plan a break at the café.

script.speaker: Mariela
script.text: Fine, but can we set a timer? I only need about ten minutes to lay everything out.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: Sure, ten minutes it is. Then we can reward ourselves with that café visit.

meta.seed_scenario: One person made a joke at the other’s expense in public, leading to embarrassment and hurt, even after being asked to stop.
meta.selected_scenario: After a neighborhood meeting, one person undermines another's contributions, suggesting they are not as valuable as they think.

script.speaker: Dat
script.text: Hey Quoc, about that meeting last night... I felt like my ideas on the park cleanup got brushed aside.

script.speaker: Quoc
script.text: Brushed aside? I mean, you did suggest using balloons for the kids. Not everyone thought that was a great fit.

script.speaker: Dat
script.text: I was just trying to add some fun to it. But it felt like you really didn't take my suggestions seriously.

script.speaker: Quoc
script.text: Look, I get it, but you know how these meetings can go. We have to focus on what’s practical, right?

script.speaker: Dat
script.text: Practical doesn’t mean dismissing good ideas. We could’ve had a little creativity, you know?

script.speaker: Quoc
script.text: Creativity is great, but let's not forget, I’m the one organizing the whole thing. I have to think about what works.

script.speaker: Dat
script.text: Right, and I appreciate that, but it just feels like you think you know better than the rest of us.

script.speaker: Quoc
script.text: It’s not about knowing better, Dat. It’s about getting things done. You’re not the only one who has to deal with this.

script.speaker: Dat
script.text: I know that, but a little respect for my input wouldn’t hurt. It’s a community event after all.

script.speaker: Quoc
script.text: Sure, but sometimes you just gotta accept that not every idea gets to fly. That’s how it works.

script.speaker: Dat
script.text: Fine, but don’t expect me to just sit back next time.

script.speaker: Quoc
script.text: Okay, we'll see how that goes. Just remember who’s actually running the show.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone shares something they found uplifting, and the other responds with cynicism or mockery, dulling the moment.
meta.selected_scenario: After a friend expresses their desire to explore a new hobby for personal growth, the other scoffs, suggesting it's a waste of time.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: So, you really think you wanna start a pottery class? I mean, don't you have enough going on already?

script.speaker: Demetrius
script.text: Yeah, but that’s exactly why I want to do it. I need a break from all the stress. It's something creative.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: I get that, but pottery? Seems a bit... I don’t know, pointless? I mean, what’s the endgame here?

script.speaker: Demetrius
script.text: It’s not about an endgame. It’s just something that makes me happy. You wouldn’t understand if you don’t try.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: But you could be focusing on more practical things. Like, I don't know, your career or, like, saving up for something.

script.speaker: Demetrius
script.text: Why does it have to be one or the other? Can't I do something just for fun? It’s not like I’m blowing all my savings on this.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Well, I just think you should be careful about wasting time on stuff that won't help you in the long run. You know how tough things are.

script.speaker: Demetrius
script.text: I know things are tough, and I’m aware of my priorities, but this isn’t wasting time. It’s self-care.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Self-care? I mean, it sounds good in theory, but what if it just turns out to be a phase? You know how you are.

script.speaker: Demetrius
script.text: Like I said, you don't get it. Just because it's not your thing doesn’t mean it’s not for me.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: I just want you to be realistic, man. I care about you and don’t want to see you get sidetracked.

script.speaker: Demetrius
script.text: Yeah, well maybe let me decide what sidetracked means for me, alright?

meta.seed_scenario: Someone tries to offer 'helpful' advice by steering the other person toward a path they didn’t choose, assuming they know what’s best.
meta.selected_scenario: One sibling shares their aspirations, and the other responds with skepticism, suggesting they should pursue a more conventional path.

script.speaker: Latoya
script.text: So, I’ve been thinking about applying to that art school in New York. It’s a big step, but I feel like it’s what I really want.

script.speaker: Ngoc
script.text: Art school? Really? I mean, what’s the plan after that? It’s not exactly a solid career move.

script.speaker: Latoya
script.text: I don’t know yet, but it’s about following my passion. I can’t just keep doing something I hate.

script.speaker: Ngoc
script.text: But look at Mom and Dad, they want us to be stable. You know how hard it was for them to come here and—

script.speaker: Latoya
script.text: Yeah, but that’s their dream, not mine! I’m not just gonna take some boring desk job because it’s safe.

script.speaker: Ngoc
script.text: Safe is good! You know, not everyone can just chase dreams all the time. Some of us have to be realistic.

script.speaker: Latoya
script.text: Realistic doesn’t mean giving up on what I love. I’ve worked too hard to just settle.

script.speaker: Ngoc
script.text: I get that, but what if it doesn’t work out? You’ll end up regretting it, and then what? You’ll be stuck with nothing.

script.speaker: Latoya
script.text: At least I’d be trying, instead of just playing it safe. You act like I’m making some huge mistake.

script.speaker: Ngoc
script.text: I just think you should think it through. You can’t just jump into something without a backup plan.

script.speaker: Latoya
script.text: I don’t need a backup plan! I need to be true to myself for once.

script.speaker: Ngoc
script.text: Fine, but don’t come crying to me later when it all blows up in your face.

meta.seed_scenario: One person goes through a difficult time but feels the other isn’t showing up—whether emotionally, practically, or with their presence.
meta.selected_scenario: A subordinate seeks guidance on a challenging task, but the superior is preoccupied and offers only vague advice.

script.speaker: Xuan
script.text: Hey Kimberly, I wanted to talk about the report. I’m really struggling with the data analysis part.

script.speaker: Kimberly
script.text: Uh, yeah, I can see that. Just make sure you focus on the key metrics we discussed last week.

script.speaker: Xuan
script.text: Right, but I’m not sure how to pull out those metrics effectively. I could use a bit more guidance.

script.speaker: Kimberly
script.text: Well, you should just look at the trends. It’s pretty straightforward if you follow the template.

script.speaker: Xuan
script.text: I get that, but the template isn’t clear on a few points, and I feel like I’m missing something.

script.speaker: Kimberly
script.text: Look, Xuan, I really need you to take initiative on this. I’m swamped with other projects.

script.speaker: Xuan
script.text: I understand you’re busy, but this is crucial for our presentation next week. I just need a little more input.

script.speaker: Kimberly
script.text: I can’t hold your hand through this. Just try to work with what you have. You’ll figure it out.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone seeks a calm environment to recharge, but the other brings in chaos—noise, drama, interruptions—without considering that the other person needs.
meta.selected_scenario: During a crucial meeting, one person tries to focus on the agenda, but the other frequently interrupts with unrelated comments.

script.speaker: Mariela
script.text: Can we get back to the agenda, Wei? We still need to cover the budget proposal.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: Oh, right, but you know, I was just thinking about how last year’s predictions were completely off, right? Like, we didn’t even account for the staffing changes.

script.speaker: Mariela
script.text: I get that, but if we keep going off on these tangents, we won't finish in time. We need to stick to the points on the agenda.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: But those changes are relevant! If we don’t address them, we might end up in the same situation. Plus, other departments are asking questions.

script.speaker: Mariela
script.text: I understand other departments are asking, but we can’t solve everything in this meeting. Maybe we can set up a separate discussion for that?

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: A separate meeting? I mean, you might be pushing that too much. We should be able to address it here since this is a critical point.

script.speaker: Mariela
script.text: Right, but if we keep dragging this out, we risk missing the deadline for the budget submission. It's just about prioritizing.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: I hear you, but honestly, I think you underestimate the impact of those staffing changes. They could derail everything if we don’t factor them in.

script.speaker: Mariela
script.text: Maybe, but if we don't at least finalize this budget proposal, we won't have anything to work with. It’s a matter of urgency.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: Urgency is one thing, but being thorough is another. I thought we were aiming for quality, not just speed.

script.speaker: Mariela
script.text: It's not just about speed, Wei. It's about balance. We can’t afford to be stuck on just one issue.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: Fine, but don’t expect me to just drop this. I’ll bring it up later if we have to.
